result.markdown:
--- title: "Cross-Dataset Extraction Validation" subtitle: "Davies et al. (2020) vs Jones & DuVal (2019) — shared effect sizes" author: "Eduardo S. A. Santos & Collaborators" date: today format: html: toc: true toc-depth: 3 theme: cosmo code-fold: true code-summary: "Show R code" self-contained: true --- ## What this document validates We are updating a mate-choice-copying meta-analysis built on **two existing datasets that overlap in the primary studies they cover but report different effect-size metrics**: * **Davies et al. (2020)** — Hedges' *d* * **Jones & DuVal (2019)** — log odds ratio (lnOR) Before pooling them we need to know: *for the studies both teams extracted, do the effect sizes agree once placed on a common scale?* This is a **method-comparison** problem (two "instruments" measuring the same underlying effects), so — unlike a single-team re-extraction audit — we can apply formal agreement statistics (correlation, Bland–Altman limits of agreement) on top of the per-study qualitative review. The matching itself (study-level crosswalk + within-study optimal effect-size assignment) is performed by [`crosscheck_davies_jones.py`](crosscheck_davies_jones.py); this report reads its output and assesses agreement. Key methodological points carried over: * **Scale conversion.** Jones lnOR is mapped to Hedges' *d* via the logistic-to-normal approximation $d = \text{lnOR}\times\sqrt{3}/\pi$ (≈ 0.5513; Borenstein et al. 2009). * **Absolute magnitudes.** The two datasets use opposite sign conventions (Davies' non-directional *d* carries the raw mean-difference sign; Jones' lnOR sign encodes copying direction), so agreement is assessed on $|d|$. Sign concordance is reported separately as a diagnostic. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Resolve the outputs dir whether rendered standalone (cwd = this file's dir) # or as part of the book (cwd = project root, via execute-dir: project). out_dir <- "outputs/3_original_dataset_crosscheck" if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) out_dir <- file.path("..", "..", out_dir) xwalk <- read_csv(file.path(out_dir, "crosswalk_effect_sizes.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE) shared <- read_csv(file.path(out_dir, "shared_studies.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE) unmatched <- read_csv(file.path(out_dir, "unmatched_jones_studies.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE) # study tag + agreement / structural-cause classification xwalk <- xwalk %>% mutate( study = paste0(author_prefix, year), agreement = if_else(magnitude_match, "agree", "disagree"), es_structure = if_else(n_jones_es == n_davies_es, "same ES count", "ES count differs"), cause = case_when( magnitude_match ~ "agree", n_jones_es != n_davies_es ~ "ES-count / extraction structure differs", TRUE ~ "same structure, magnitude conflict" ) ) ``` ::: ## Headline summary ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} n_shared <- nrow(shared) n_pairs <- nrow(xwalk) n_match <- sum(xwalk$magnitude_match) n_sign <- sum(xwalk$sign_agree) tibble( Metric = c("Shared studies (in both datasets)", "Jones studies NOT in Davies", "Comparable effect-size pairs", "Pairs matching in magnitude (|Δd| ≤ 0.15 or ≤ 15%)", "Pairs with concordant sign"), Value = c(n_shared, nrow(unmatched), n_pairs, sprintf("%d (%s%%)", n_match, fmt(100*n_match/n_pairs, 0)), sprintf("%d (%s%%)", n_sign, fmt(100*n_sign/n_pairs, 0))) ) %>% kable() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} |Metric |Value | |:------------------------------------------------------------|:--------| |Shared studies (in both datasets) |29 | |Jones studies NOT in Davies |8 | |Comparable effect-size pairs |52 | |Pairs matching in magnitude (|Δd| ≤ 0.15 or ≤ 15%) |34 (65%) | |Pairs with concordant sign |36 (69%) | ::: ::: ## Method-comparison agreement statistics ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} pear <- cor.test(xwalk$abs_jones_d, xwalk$abs_davies_d, method = "pearson") spear <- suppressWarnings( cor.test(xwalk$abs_jones_d, xwalk$abs_davies_d, method = "spearman")) fit <- lm(abs_davies_d ~ abs_jones_d, data = xwalk) # same-ES-count subset (like-for-like extractions) sub <- filter(xwalk, es_structure == "same ES count") pear_sub <- cor.test(sub$abs_jones_d, sub$abs_davies_d) # Bland-Altman on |d| xwalk <- xwalk %>% mutate(ba_mean = (abs_jones_d + abs_davies_d)/2, ba_diff = abs_jones_d - abs_davies_d) bias <- mean(xwalk$ba_diff); s <- sd(xwalk$ba_diff) loa_lo <- bias - 1.96*s; loa_hi <- bias + 1.96*s tibble( Statistic = c("Pearson r (all pairs)", "Spearman ρ (all pairs)", "Pearson r (same-ES-count studies only)", "OLS slope (Davies ~ Jones)", "OLS intercept", "Bland–Altman mean bias (Jones − Davies, |d|)", "95% limits of agreement"), Value = c(sprintf("%s (95%% CI %s–%s, %s)", fmt(pear$estimate,3), fmt(pear$conf.int[1],2), fmt(pear$conf.int[2],2), ifelse(pear$p.value < 1e-4, "p < 0.0001", paste0("p = ", fmt(pear$p.value,4)))), fmt(spear$estimate,3), sprintf("%s (n = %d pairs)", fmt(pear_sub$estimate,3), nrow(sub)), fmt(coef(fit)[2],3), fmt(coef(fit)[1],3), sprintf("%s d-units", fmt(bias,3)), sprintf("%s to %s", fmt(loa_lo,2), fmt(loa_hi,2))) ) %>% kable() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} |Statistic |Value | |:------------------------------------------------------|:------------------------------------| |Pearson r (all pairs) |0.795 (95% CI 0.67–0.88, p < 0.0001) | |Spearman ρ (all pairs) |0.862 | |Pearson r (same-ES-count studies only) |0.891 (n = 12 pairs) | |OLS slope (Davies ~ Jones) |1.193 | |OLS intercept |0.036 | |Bland–Altman mean bias (Jones − Davies, |d|) |-0.141 d-units | |95% limits of agreement |-1.05 to 0.77 | ::: ::: A negative mean bias (-0.14) and an OLS slope > 1 (1.19) both indicate Davies tends to report **larger** magnitudes than the Jones-converted values, increasingly so for larger effects. ### Agreement plots ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} lim <- c(0, max(xwalk$abs_jones_d, xwalk$abs_davies_d) * 1.05) p_scatter <- ggplot(xwalk, aes(abs_jones_d, abs_davies_d)) + geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") + geom_point(aes(colour = agreement, shape = es_structure), size = 2.6, alpha = .85) + geom_text(aes(label = study), size = 2.4, vjust = -0.8, check_overlap = TRUE) + scale_colour_manual(values = c(agree = "#2c7fb8", disagree = "#d95f0e")) + coord_equal(xlim = lim, ylim = lim) + labs(x = "Jones & DuVal |d| (lnOR × √3/π)", y = "Davies |Hedges d|", colour = NULL, shape = NULL, title = "Effect-size agreement") + theme_minimal(base_size = 12) + theme(legend.position = "bottom") p_ba <- ggplot(xwalk, aes(ba_mean, ba_diff)) + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey80") + geom_hline(yintercept = bias, colour = "#2c7fb8") + geom_hline(yintercept = c(loa_lo, loa_hi), linetype = "dashed", colour = "#d95f0e") + geom_point(aes(colour = agreement), size = 2.6, alpha = .85) + geom_text(aes(label = study), size = 2.4, vjust = -0.8, check_overlap = TRUE) + scale_colour_manual(values = c(agree = "#2c7fb8", disagree = "#d95f0e"), guide = "none") + labs(x = "Mean of the two |d|", y = "Jones − Davies (|d|)", title = "Bland–Altman") + theme_minimal(base_size = 12) p_scatter + p_ba ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Left: Jones-converted vs Davies |d| with the line of identity (dashed). Right: Bland–Altman plot (mean bias solid, 95% limits of agreement dashed). Points are coloured by whether the pair matches in magnitude; studies where the two teams extracted different numbers of effect sizes are ringed.](extraction_validation_files/figure-html/fig-agreement-1.png){#fig-agreement width=1056} ::: ::: ## Discrepancy classification Borrowing the per-study "required action" structure from Sánchez-Tójar et al.'s validation codebook, every pair is classified by *why* it does or does not agree. The dominant structural cause is whether the two teams extracted the **same number of effect sizes** from a paper. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} xwalk %>% count(cause, name = "pairs") %>% mutate(`%` = fmt(100*pairs/sum(pairs), 0)) %>% kable() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} |cause | pairs|% | |:---------------------------------------|-----:|:--| |ES-count / extraction structure differs | 13|25 | |agree | 34|65 | |same structure, magnitude conflict | 5|10 | ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} xwalk %>% transmute(study, jones_code, jones_behaviour, davies_study, davies_behaviour, davies_n, `Jones |d|` = round(abs_jones_d, 2), `Davies |d|` = round(abs_davies_d, 2), `Δ|d|` = round(abs_diff, 2), sign_agree, cause) %>% datatable(rownames = FALSE, filter = "top", options = list(pageLength = 15, scrollX = TRUE), caption = "Per-pair crosswalk. Note the behaviour columns: a Jones 'reversal/random' contrast paired against a Davies 'Affiliation/Mating' contrast flags a different OUTCOME extracted from the same paper.") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Studies flagged for manual re-extraction The clearest validation failures are pairs that **disagree despite both teams extracting the same number of effect sizes** (so the disagreement is not merely structural), plus any same-paper pair whose outcome construct differs. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} xwalk %>% filter(!magnitude_match) %>% arrange(desc(abs(abs_diff))) %>% transmute(study, jones_behaviour, davies_behaviour, `Jones |d|` = round(abs_jones_d, 2), `Davies |d|` = round(abs_davies_d, 2), `Δ|d|` = round(abs_diff, 2), n_jones_es, n_davies_es, cause) %>% kable() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} |study |jones_behaviour |davies_behaviour | Jones |d|| Davies |d|| Δ|d|| n_jones_es| n_davies_es|cause | |:-------|:---------------|:----------------|-------------------:|--------------------:|--------------:|----------:|-----------:|:---------------------------------------| |DUG1992 |reversal |Affiliation | 1.23| 3.73| -2.50| 3| 2|ES-count / extraction structure differs | |DUG1993 |random |Affiliation | 0.96| 2.38| -1.42| 2| 2|same structure, magnitude conflict | |DUG1993 |random |Affiliation | 0.22| 1.00| -0.78| 2| 2|same structure, magnitude conflict | |GIE2018 |reversal |Affiliation | 1.68| 2.43| -0.75| 4| 2|ES-count / extraction structure differs | |APP2000 |reversal |Affiliation | 0.83| 0.25| 0.58| 2| 4|ES-count / extraction structure differs | |BRI1996 |reversal |Affiliation | 0.83| 1.38| -0.55| 2| 2|same structure, magnitude conflict | |BRI1996 |reversal |Affiliation | 0.83| 1.38| -0.55| 2| 2|same structure, magnitude conflict | |HOW1998 |reversal |Affiliation | 0.61| 1.14| -0.53| 1| 1|same structure, magnitude conflict | |FOW2015 |model_choice |Mating | 0.10| 0.63| -0.53| 2| 1|ES-count / extraction structure differs | |APP2000 |same |Affiliation | 0.74| 0.24| 0.49| 2| 4|ES-count / extraction structure differs | |NOB2018 |less_attractive |Mating | 0.60| 0.13| 0.47| 2| 4|ES-count / extraction structure differs | |GOD2009 |random |Affiliation | 1.88| 1.52| 0.35| 1| 2|ES-count / extraction structure differs | |GIE2018 |reversal |Affiliation | 1.68| 1.98| -0.30| 4| 2|ES-count / extraction structure differs | |DAN2018 |random |Mating | 0.80| 1.03| -0.23| 17| 2|ES-count / extraction structure differs | |DRU2008 |reversal |Affiliation | 0.56| 0.76| -0.21| 1| 2|ES-count / extraction structure differs | |DUG2003 |reversal |Affiliation | 0.93| 1.13| -0.20| 2| 4|ES-count / extraction structure differs | |MER2009 |random |Mating | 0.49| 0.68| -0.19| 2| 3|ES-count / extraction structure differs | |GER2016 |random |Mating latency | 0.60| 0.45| 0.15| 4| 8|ES-count / extraction structure differs | ::: ::: ## Sign-concordance diagnostic Because the sign conventions differ by construction, low sign agreement is *expected* and is not itself a validation failure — but a breakdown helps confirm the conventions are simply inverted rather than randomly assigned. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} xwalk %>% summarise(`pairs` = n(), `sign agree` = sum(sign_agree), `% agree` = fmt(100*mean(sign_agree), 0)) %>% kable() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} | pairs| sign agree|% agree | |-----:|----------:|:-------| | 52| 36|69 | ::: ::: ## Effect-size-count reconciliation How the two teams partitioned each shared paper into effect sizes (a major driver of the comparable-pair count, since only `min(nJ, nD)` pairs per study can be aligned). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} shared %>% transmute(study = paste0(author_prefix, year), jones_species, davies_species, n_jones_es, n_davies_es, n_compared, `unpaired` = abs(n_jones_es - n_davies_es)) %>% arrange(desc(unpaired)) %>% datatable(rownames = FALSE, options = list(pageLength = 15, scrollX = TRUE)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} shared %>% summarise( `Jones effect sizes (shared studies)` = sum(n_jones_es), `Davies effect sizes (shared studies)` = sum(n_davies_es), `Comparable pairs (Σ min)` = sum(n_compared)) %>% pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Count") %>% kable() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} |Metric | Count| |:------------------------------------|-----:| |Jones effect sizes (shared studies) | 75| |Davies effect sizes (shared studies) | 87| |Comparable pairs (Σ min) | 52| ::: ::: ## Jones studies absent from Davies Verified against **all** Davies studies (matching species epithet anywhere, author prefix at any year, and year ± 1); none have a Davies counterpart. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} unmatched %>% transmute(study = paste0(author_prefix, year), species, n_effect_sizes, reason) %>% kable() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} |study |species | n_effect_sizes|reason | |:-------|:------------|--------------:|:----------------------------------------------| |DUG1996 |reticulata | 4|no Davies study with this author-prefix + year | |FRE2009 |ater | 2|no Davies study with this author-prefix + year | |GOD2005 |latipinna | 2|no Davies study with this author-prefix + year | |GOU1997 |leucogaster | 2|no Davies study with this author-prefix + year | |GOU2006 |leucogaster | 2|no Davies study with this author-prefix + year | |MON2018 |melanogaster | 4|no Davies study with this author-prefix + year | |MON2019 |melanogaster | 10|no Davies study with this author-prefix + year | |STI2011 |olmstedi | 2|no Davies study with this author-prefix + year | ::: ::: ## What to report in the manuscript * **Overlap.** Of 37 Jones & DuVal studies, **29 are shared** with Davies; 8 are unique to Jones (28 effect sizes). * **Agreement.** Across 52 comparable effect-size pairs, Jones-converted and Davies magnitudes correlate at Pearson *r* = 0.79 (65% within tolerance), rising to *r* = 0.89 among studies where both teams extracted the same number of effect sizes. * **Direction of disagreement.** Mean Bland–Altman bias = -0.14 *d*-units, indicating Davies reports slightly larger magnitudes on average; most large discrepancies trace to **different outcomes extracted** (behaviour-type columns) or **different effect-size partitioning**, not to the metric conversion. ```
